heading: Enhancing State and local efforts to combat trafficking in persons

children.heading: Establishment of grant program for law enforcement
children.label: a Establishment of grant program for law enforcement
A
children.text: to investigate and prosecute acts of severe forms of trafficking in persons, and related offenses that occur, in whole or in part, within the territorial jurisdiction of the United States;

children.label: B
children.text: to train law enforcement personnel how to identify victims of severe forms of trafficking in persons and related offenses;

children.label: C
children.text: to investigate and prosecute persons who engage in the purchase of commercial sex acts and prioritize the investigations and prosecutions of those cases involving minor victims;

children.label: D
children.text: to educate persons charged with, or convicted of, purchasing or attempting to purchase commercial sex acts;

children.label: E
children.text: to educate and train law enforcement personnel in how to establish trust of persons subjected to trafficking and encourage cooperation with prosecution efforts; and

children.label: F
children.text: as appropriate, to designate at least 1 prosecutor for cases of severe forms of trafficking in persons (as such term is defined in section 7102(9) of title 22).1See References in Text note below.1

children.heading: Definition
children.label: 2 Definition
children.text: In this subsection, the term “related offenses” includes violations of tax laws, transacting in illegally derived proceeds, money laundering, racketeering, and other violations of criminal laws committed in connection with an act of sex trafficking or a severe form of trafficking in persons.

children.heading: Multi-disciplinary approach required
children.label: b Multi-disciplinary approach required
children.text: Grants under subsection (a) may be made only for programs in which the State or local law enforcement agency works collaboratively with social service providers and relevant nongovernmental organizations, including organizations with experience in the delivery of services to persons who are the subject of trafficking in persons.

children.heading: Limitation on Federal share
children.label: c Limitation on Federal share
children.text: The Federal share of a grant made under this section may not exceed 75 percent of the total costs of the projects described in the application submitted.

children.heading: No limitation on section 20702 grant applications
children.label: d No limitation on section 20702 grant applications
children.text: An entity that applies for a grant under is not prohibited from also applying for a grant under this section.section 20702 of this title

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: e Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated to the Attorney General to carry out this section $10,000,000 for each of the fiscal years 2014 through 2021.

the ability of law enforcement personnel to identify victims of severe forms of trafficking in persons and investigate and prosecute cases against offenders, including offenders who engage in the purchasing of commercial sex acts with a minor; and
recommendations, if any, regarding any legislative or administrative action the Comptroller General determines appropriate to improve the ability described in paragraph (1).
